_comment: TDS — Scott Gross Caricature Artist — Pre-Crime deployment for lead capture and marketplace posting

product.name: Live Caricature Entertainment
product.description: Scott Gross is a professional caricature artist available for corporate events, weddings, bar/bat mitzvahs, private parties, and trade shows in the Los Angeles area. He draws fast, funny, flattering likenesses on the spot — guests leave with a keepsake they actually want to keep.
differentiators: Fast, clean style — no exaggerated grotesques. Guests look like themselves, just funnier.
Works any event size from 20 to 500+ guests
Fully self-contained — easel, paper, markers. No setup needs from the venue.
Available as digital caricatures (iPad) for instant-share events
product.geography: Los Angeles metro area — Orange County, Ventura County on request

factletTopics: corporate event planning trends Los Angeles

criteria.0: No contact found or no email
criteria.1: Generic inbox (info@, events@) or role unclear

criteria.0: No evidence of event planning or entertainment need
criteria.1: Historical evidence (past events, past entertainment bookings)
criteria.2: Active signal (announcing event, asking for recommendations, posting about planning)

criteria.1: Event type known but no date or size
criteria.2: Event type + date or size known — bookable intel

criteria.0: No useful intel (no social, no site, no news)
criteria.1: Scraped something useful — event details, budget signals, past bookings

criteria.0: Only generic info — no differentiating facts
criteria.1: At least one fact unique to THIS client (named event, specific date, stated need)

criteria.0: Nothing specific enough to open with
criteria.1: Clear hook exists — specific event, stated need, or recent post

hardGates: No email at all → warmthScore = 0, draftStatus = brewing, do not compose
Outside LA metro → warmthScore = 0, mark brewing, log OUT_OF_GEOGRAPHY

evaluatorCriteria.name: Specificity
evaluatorCriteria.description: Does the draft reference something specific to THIS event or person — not 'events like yours'?
evaluatorCriteria.passExample: References a named event, stated date, specific type of gathering unique to them
evaluatorCriteria.failExample: 'Companies often book entertainment for...' / 'Events like yours benefit from...'

evaluatorCriteria.name: Recency
evaluatorCriteria.description: Is the hook recent — based on something they said, posted, or announced recently?
evaluatorCriteria.passExample: References a post, announcement, or event within the past 60 days
evaluatorCriteria.failExample: Only references static facts with no timely hook

evaluatorCriteria.name: Pain-to-Product Bridge
evaluatorCriteria.description: Does the draft connect THEIR event need to caricature entertainment in one clear sentence?
evaluatorCriteria.passExample: One sentence: their event type → caricature artist = the entertainment that actually sticks
evaluatorCriteria.failExample: Describes Scott's skills without connecting to their specific event

evaluatorCriteria.name: Brevity
evaluatorCriteria.description: Under 120 words. Every sentence earns its place.
evaluatorCriteria.passExample: Tight, punchy, nothing wasted. No filler.
evaluatorCriteria.failExample: Over 120 words, or contains any forbidden phrase

evaluatorCriteria.name: Reply Test
evaluatorCriteria.description: Would a busy event planner reply?
evaluatorCriteria.passExample: They forward it to the person booking entertainment
evaluatorCriteria.failExample: Finger on delete by sentence two

outreachRules.tone: warm, direct, confident — not salesy. Sounds like a working artist who's done this a thousand times.
outreachRules.openWith: Lead with THEIR event — their date, their venue, their guest count. Not with Scott's name.
outreachRules.closeWith: Close with a specific ask: 'Is [date] still open?' or 'What's the guest count?' — not 'let me know if you're interested'
forbidden: I hope this finds you well

_defaultBookingAction_options: leedz_api    — post to The Leedz marketplace via API
email_user   — email to companyEmail for seller to decide
